params.name: Lamassu Statue
params.description:
On Nov 18, 1328 BC, the Lamassu guardian sculpture was placed at the entrance of the castle of Sargon II.
This stone statue depicts the guardian Lamassu of ancient Assyria. Lamassu is a monster with a human head, a bull body, and wings. It can fly and has great power. As the guardian of Assyria, he defended the Assyrian temples and palaces along with another monster named Sedu. This stone statue, which is regarded as the incarnation of Lamassu, has the effect of dispelling evil spirits and ot …
